title: hieracium thapsiforme
intro: Hieracium thapsiforme, also known as mouse-ear hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the dandelion family that grows up to 1 foot tall, typically found in grasslands and meadows. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium thapsiforme is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of Hieracium thapsiforme can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
